---
description: 'C# coding style guide'
---

# C# Coding Style

## Naming Conventions

| Type | Convention | Example |
|------|-----------|---------|
| Class/Struct/Enum | PascalCase | `UserService`, `MenuType` |
| Method | PascalCase | `GetUserAsync`, `ValidateToken` |
| Interface | I + PascalCase | `IAuthService`, `IUserService` |
| Public Property | PascalCase | `UserName`, `IsActive` |
| Private Field | _camelCase | `_logger`, `_dbContext` |
| Parameter/Local Variable | camelCase | `userId`, `accessToken` |
| Constant | PascalCase | `MaxRetryCount`, `DefaultPageSize` |
| Async Method | Add `Async` suffix | `LoginAsync`, `GetMenusAsync` |

## File Structure

- Use file-scoped namespaces (`namespace X;`)
- One primary type per file
- `using` order: System → Microsoft → Third-party → Project-internal

## Code Design

### Least Exposure Principle

`private` > `internal` > `protected` > `public`

### Null Handling

```csharp
// ✅ Correct
if (user is null) return;
if (name is not null) { ... }
ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(parameter);

// ❌ Wrong
if (user == null) return;
if (name != null) { ... }
```

### Strings

```csharp
// ✅ Correct
if (name == string.Empty) { ... }
var result = string.Empty;

// ❌ Wrong
if (name == "") { ... }
var result = "";
```

### DTOs and Immutability

- Prefer `record` for DTOs
- Prefer `init` over `set` for properties
- Mark fields as `readonly`

### Method Quality

- **Methods should not exceed 20 lines** (excluding blank lines and comments)
- **Nesting should not exceed 3 levels** — use guard clauses / early return
- Each method should do one thing

### Modern C# Features (C# 12+)

- Primary constructors
- Switch expressions
- Expression body members
- Collection expressions
- Pattern matching

### Structured Logging

```csharp
// ✅ Correct — placeholder syntax
_logger.LogInformation("User {UserId} logged in", userId);

// ❌ Wrong — string interpolation
_logger.LogInformation($"User {userId} logged in");
```

### Exception Handling

- Use precise exception types (`ArgumentException`, `InvalidOperationException`)
- Do not throw or catch base `Exception`
- Do not swallow errors — log and re-throw or propagate upward

### Async Guidelines

- All async methods end with `Async`
- End-to-end async — do not use sync-over-async
- Accept and pass `CancellationToken`
- Default to `Task`; only use `ValueTask` after performance measurement

## Comments

- Comments explain **why**, not what
- Do not add unnecessary comments
- Do not edit auto-generated code (`*.g.cs`, `// <auto-generated>`)
